commit.message:
fix(ghostty): pre-warm portal.Desktop and Flatpak synchronously

The previous wrapper fired StartServiceByName for org.freedesktop.Flatpak
in the background (&), which raced with ghostty's own D-Bus calls — ghostty
could still stall waiting for a cold service.

Change to synchronous pre-warm of both:
  - org.freedesktop.portal.Desktop  (xdg-desktop-portal, used for
    GlobalShortcuts and portal settings on every startup)
  - org.freedesktop.Flatpak         (flatpak-session-helper, used for
    FlatpakHostCommand to spawn shells on the host)

When both services are already running each gdbus call returns in <10 ms.
When cold they block for the activation duration (~1-3 s each) so ghostty
starts with both services provably ready, eliminating any internal timeout.

Also update GOTCHAS.md with corrected dual-service root cause, improved
diagnostics, and a note about the host-level portal backend conflict case.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: resilient usage display under API rate limiting (#193)

* fix: resilient usage display under API rate limiting

The Anthropic usage API rate-limits to ~1 call per 5 minutes. With the
previous 60s cache TTL, 4 out of 5 API calls returned 429, causing the
HUD to permanently display "(429)" instead of actual usage data.

Three-layer fix:
- Increase cache TTL from 60s to 5 minutes to match rate limit window
- Preserve lastGoodData in cache across rate-limited periods so the HUD
  always shows the best available data instead of errors
- Exponential backoff (60s→120s→240s→5min cap) with Retry-After header
  support for consecutive 429 responses

Also show "syncing..." instead of raw HTTP status on first-run rate limit.

* Update usage-api.ts

commit.message:
Rename misleading input_rank param to input_dim_value in processSlice… (#7739)

This pull request improves the handling of the `Slice` operator when
applied to empty or scalar-shaped tensors, ensuring correct shape
inference and preventing potential crashes. The changes include both a
bug fix in the shape inference logic and the addition of a targeted test
case.

**Bug fix for `Slice` shape inference:**

* Updated the `processSliceInputs` function in `defs.cc` to use the
actual input dimension size or value (instead of input rank) when
calculating slice bounds, ensuring correct handling for empty or scalar
dimensions.

**Testing improvements:**

* Added a new test `test_slice_scalar_shape_output` in
`shape_inference_test.py` to verify that slicing the shape of a scalar
tensor produces a zero-length output and does not crash.

---------

Signed-off-by: Ti-Tai Wang <titaiwang@microsoft.com>

Instant WS notifications, checkmark repositioned, rounding fix

  tx_notification, then refresh in background for full details. This
  gives sub-second transaction visibility instead of waiting for the
  HTTP round-trip.
- Move status checkmark to last item in transaction row (after
  amount/time) on both OverviewScreen and TransactionHistoryScreen.

commit.message:
fix: defensive truncation gates prevent UBSAN on truncated profiles

iccanalyzer-lite links UNPATCHED iccDEV and must handle all security
findings through defensive programming — never library patches.

Truncated profiles (header.size > actual file size) cause UBSAN when
CIccProfile::Read() eagerly loads tags whose data lies beyond EOF.
Invalid enum values in calculator elements trigger CWE-681.

Three truncation gates added:
1. IccAnalyzerSecurity.cpp: skipLibraryPhase when H1 detects truncation
2. IccAnalyzerValidation.cpp: RoundTripAnalyze() skips Read() on truncation
3. IccAnalyzerComprehensive.cpp: phases 3-5 skip on truncation

IsProfileTruncated() helper reads first 4 bytes (BE profile size) and
compares against stat() file size — lightweight, no library dependency.

Verified: 230/230 tests pass, 0 ASAN/UBSAN on crash PoCs including
AFL-found truncated v5 spac profile (2267 vs 3928 bytes).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(ghostty): pre-warm D-Bus portal to eliminate 10s first-launch delay

Ghostty is built with -Dflatpak=true, which enables an internal D-Bus
bridge to org.freedesktop.Flatpak for host-process spawning. On first
launch after boot, if flatpak-session-helper is not yet running, D-Bus
must cold-start it synchronously — taking ~10 seconds.

Add ghostty-wrapper.sh as the Flatpak command. It fires a non-blocking
gdbus StartServiceByName call for org.freedesktop.Flatpak in the
background before exec-ing ghostty, giving the portal service a head
start so it is ready by the time ghostty needs it.

If the delay persists after this fix, it is likely GTK4 shader
compilation; test with GSK_RENDERER=cairo and add --env=GSK_RENDERER=gl
to finish-args if confirmed. Diagnosis commands documented in GOTCHAS.md.
